Azure.Core

### Describe the bug

# Description
We have a [file here](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/scripts/splittestdependencies/tests/inputs/projects.txt) which is supposed to list all the test projects that need to be split into jobs and run during a core ci run.

It appears this file is manually maintained and has deviated from reality if I understand its purpose correctly.

Currently there are 129 track 2 test csproj files that were in the repo but not in the split file.
There are also 6 test csproj files that were in the split file but not in the repo

We might consider auto-generating this file and failing in ci if there is a diff with the checked in version to ensure these stay in sync.

Below are the details of those files

```C#
namespace splitCheck
{
    internal class Program
    {
        static void Main(string[] args)
        {
            string repoMarker = "$(RepoRoot)";
            string repoRoot = @"D:\Git\azure4\azure-sdk-for-net";
            HashSet<string> splitFiles = new HashSet<string>(File.ReadLines($@"{repoRoot}\eng\scripts\splittestdependencies\tests\inputs\projects.txt"));
            HashSet<string> testProjFiles = new HashSet<string>();
            var root = new DirectoryInfo($@"{repoRoot}\sdk");
            foreach (var serviceDir in root.GetDirectories())
            {
                foreach (var projectDir in serviceDir.GetDirectories())
                {
                    if (!projectDir.FullName.Contains(@"\Azure.", StringComparison.Ordinal))
                        continue;
                    foreach (var subDir in projectDir.GetDirectories("*", SearchOption.AllDirectories))
                    {
                        if (subDir.FullName.EndsWith(@"\.vs", StringComparison.Ordinal) || subDir.FullName.Contains(@"\.vs\", StringComparison.Ordinal))
                            continue;
                        if (!subDir.FullName.EndsWith(@"\tests", StringComparison.Ordinal) && !subDir.FullName.Contains(@"\tests\", StringComparison.Ordinal))
                            continue;
                        var csprojFiles = subDir.GetFiles("*.csproj");
                        if (csprojFiles.Length == 0)
                            continue;
                        testProjFiles.Add(csprojFiles[0].FullName.Replace($@"{repoRoot}\", repoMarker, StringComparison.Ordinal));
                    }
                }
            }

            Console.WriteLine("Files that exist in the repo but aren't in the split file");
            int missingInSplit = 0;
            foreach (var file in testProjFiles)
            {
                if (!splitFiles.Contains(file))
                {
                    Console.WriteLine(file);
                    missingInSplit++;
                }
            }
            Console.WriteLine();
            Console.WriteLine("Files that exist in the split file but aren't in the repo");
            int missingInRepo = 0;
            foreach (var file in splitFiles)
            {
                if (!testProjFiles.Contains(file) && !File.Exists(file.Replace(repoMarker, $@"{repoRoot}\", StringComparison.Ordinal)))
                {
                    Console.WriteLine(file);
                    missingInRepo++;
                }
            }

            Console.WriteLine($"{missingInSplit} files were in the repo but not in the split file");
            Console.WriteLine($"{missingInRepo} files were in the split file but not in the repo");
        }
    }
}
```

### Expected behavior

The file and reality should match

### Actual behavior

There are differences on both sides

### Reproduction Steps

I wrote a quick script to validate

```C#
namespace splitCheck
{
    internal class Program
    {
        static void Main(string[] args)
        {
            string repoMarker = "$(RepoRoot)";
            string repoRoot = @"D:\Git\azure4\azure-sdk-for-net";
            HashSet<string> splitFiles = new HashSet<string>(File.ReadLines($@"{repoRoot}\eng\scripts\splittestdependencies\tests\inputs\projects.txt"));
            HashSet<string> testProjFiles = new HashSet<string>();
            var root = new DirectoryInfo($@"{repoRoot}\sdk");
            foreach (var serviceDir in root.GetDirectories())
            {
                foreach (var projectDir in serviceDir.GetDirectories())
                {
                    if (!projectDir.FullName.Contains(@"\Azure.", StringComparison.Ordinal))
                        continue;
                    foreach (var subDir in projectDir.GetDirectories("*", SearchOption.AllDirectories))
                    {
                        if (subDir.FullName.EndsWith(@"\.vs", StringComparison.Ordinal) || subDir.FullName.Contains(@"\.vs\", StringComparison.Ordinal))
                            continue;
                        if (!subDir.FullName.EndsWith(@"\tests", StringComparison.Ordinal) && !subDir.FullName.Contains(@"\tests\", StringComparison.Ordinal))
                            continue;
                        var csprojFiles = subDir.GetFiles("*.csproj");
                        if (csprojFiles.Length == 0)
                            continue;
                        testProjFiles.Add(csprojFiles[0].FullName.Replace($@"{repoRoot}\", repoMarker, StringComparison.Ordinal));
                    }
                }
            }

            Console.WriteLine("Files that exist in the repo but aren't in the split file");
            int missingInSplit = 0;
            foreach (var file in testProjFiles)
            {
                if (!splitFiles.Contains(file))
                {
                    Console.WriteLine(file);
                    missingInSplit++;
                }
            }
            Console.WriteLine();
            Console.WriteLine("Files that exist in the split file but aren't in the repo");
            int missingInRepo = 0;
            foreach (var file in splitFiles)
            {
                if (!testProjFiles.Contains(file) && !File.Exists(file.Replace(repoMarker, $@"{repoRoot}\", StringComparison.Ordinal)))
                {
                    Console.WriteLine(file);
                    missingInRepo++;
                }
            }

            Console.WriteLine($"{missingInSplit} files were in the repo but not in the split file");
            Console.WriteLine($"{missingInRepo} files were in the split file but not in the repo");
        }
    }
}
```

### Describe the bug

When trying to use the azure Management SDK for Cosmos DB every time we use the CosmosDBAccountPatch object to say set IPRules no matter what we set the Allow Access from Azure Portal flag is set to false/disabled.

For Example:
```
var ri = CosmosDBAccountResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(IaCConfiguration.AzureSubscriptionId.ToString()
                                                     , resourceConfig.ResourceGroupName
                                                     , resourceConfig.ResourceName);

var armResource = ArmClient.GetCosmosDBAccountResource(ri);

if (armResource == null)
 {
     continue;
 }

var armResourceData = await armResource.GetAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] ConfigureAwait(false);
 if (armResourceData?. Value?. Data?. IPRules == null)
 {
     continue;
 }

var firewallRuleCollection = armResourceData.Value.Data.IPRules;

if (firewallRuleCollection == null)
 {
     continue;
 }

//Clear all existing rules
 firewallRuleCollection.Clear();

firewallRuleCollection.Add(new CosmosDBIPAddressOrRange {  IPAddressOrRange = '<IP Address>'});

var updateParameters = new CosmosDBAccountPatch();

updateParameters.NetworkAclBypass = armResourceData.Value.Data.NetworkAclBypass;
 updateParameters.PublicNetworkAccess = armResourceData.Value.Data.PublicNetworkAccess;

foreach (var ipRule in firewallRuleCollection)
 {
     updateParameters.IPRules.Add(ipRule);
 }

var updateOperation = await armResource.UpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, updateParameters, cancellationToken). ConfigureAwait(false);
```

### Expected behavior

The Allow Access from Azure Portal flag would not be mutated unless we explicitly set it.

### Actual behavior

The Allow Access from Azure Portal flag is mutated back to its default of disabled it seems..

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-generated issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql` package — specifically, the README lacks service-specific key concepts and code examples, and the upcoming CHANGELOG entry is empty despite a TypeSpec migration having occurred.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql` (latest: 1.1.0-beta.2)
**Affected API:** README, CHANGELOG, and test sample infrastructure (`tests/Samples/`)
**Scenarios:**
- "Key concepts" section in README links only to generic Azure SDK page; no description of CosmosDB for PostgreSQL resources (Cluster, Server, FirewallRule, Configuration, etc.)
- "Examples" section in README has no inline code snippets
- No `tests/Samples/` directory or `#region Snippet:` tags exist, so `Update-Snippets.ps1` cannot inject examples
- CHANGELOG for upcoming 1.1.0-beta.3 has all sections empty despite TypeSpec migration changes

**Root ask:** Add service-specific README content (key concepts + code examples with snippet tags) and update the CHANGELOG for the upcoming release.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a bot-generated documentation tracking issue triggered by commit e373db219 (PR #57114) which completed the MPG (management plane generation) migration for CosmosDB for PostgreSQL, adding `ci.mgmt.yml` and `test-resources.json`.

The implementation guide in the issue body provides detailed steps including:
- Files to modify: `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/README.md`
- File to create: `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/tests/Samples/Sample1_Clusters.cs`
- Validation commands: `dotnet build`, `dotnet test`, `Update-Snippets.ps1`, `Export-API.ps1`, `dotnet format`

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Verify the current state of README and CHANGELOG in `sdk/cosmosdbforpostgresql/Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql/`
2. Create the `tests/Samples/Sample1_Clusters.cs` file with NUnit test methods wrapped in `#region Snippet:` tags as described in the implementation guide
3. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 cosmosdbforpostgresql` to inject snippet content into README placeholders
4. Update CHANGELOG to reflect API surface changes from the TypeSpec migration
</details>

<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — The package is `Azure.ResourceManager.CosmosDBForPostgreSql`, which clearly falls under the management plane SDK category
- **Service:** `Cosmos` — CosmosDB for PostgreSQL issues in this repo use the "Cosmos" service label (confirmed by issue #37741 for a CosmosDB for PostgreSQL query)

The service label "Cosmos" is used consistently for both Azure Cosmos DB and CosmosDB for PostgreSQL issues in this repo, though a dedicated label for CosmosDB for PostgreSQL is absent from CODEOWNERS.
